title: The City That Never Slept
content: When Noah visited New York for the first time, he was amazed by the city’s constant energy. Restaurants remained open late into the night, streets stayed busy, and bright lights illuminated entire neighborhoods. Even after midnight, people continued working, socializing, and exploring. Noah spent one evening walking through several districts simply observing the atmosphere. The city seemed alive at all hours. Although the pace occasionally felt overwhelming, he appreciated the excitement and diversity surrounding him. The experience helped him understand why so many people described it as a city that never slept.
